rationale: Expands `JIRA_DC_WEBHOOK_EVENTS` in `enterprise/integrations/jira_dc/jira_dc_manager.py` from `['comment_created', 'jira:issue_updated']` to a six-event list (`jira:issue_created/updated/deleted`, `comment_created/updated/deleted`). Introduces a `_extract_workspace_url(payload)` helper that walks four candidate paths (`comment.author.self`, `user.self`, `issue.self`, `comment.self`) and replaces the conditional `selfUrl` chain in `validate_request_context` with a single call to it, so workspace identification works for the broader event set. New tests: `test_validate_request_issue_created_success` and `test_validate_request_comment_updated_success` confirm validation succeeds for the new event types and `get_workspace_by_name` is called with `jira.company.com`; a parametrized `test_parse_webhook_automation_only_events_do_not_start_resolver` asserts `parse_webhook` returns `None` for the four automation-only events (correctly preserving the narrow resolver-job trigger set the PR body promises). The existing `test_register_webhook_updates_existing_url` is updated to assert against the new `JIRA_DC_WEBHOOK_EVENTS` constant, not a hardcoded list. The mock-of-hallucination detector firing on `from openhands.app_server.integrations.service_types import RequestMethod` in test_jira_dc_service appears to be an import the detector did not recognize, but it is a sibling test file and not modified here — this is a false positive on the manager+route tests.
